Voce IR Docs Playground GitHub

The code is gone. The experience remains.

Voce IR is a binary intermediate representation for user interfaces. AI generates typed IR from natural language. A compiler emits optimized output across 7 targets. No framework. No runtime. Just the experience.

Get Started

View on GitHub

01

Conversation

Describe what you want in natural language. The AI asks questions, builds context, and pushes back on anti-patterns.

02

Generate IR

AI emits typed binary IR — 27 node types covering layout, state, animation, accessibility, SEO, and forms.

03

Validate

9 validation passes enforce 46 quality rules. Accessibility, security, and SEO are compile errors, not warnings.

04

Compile & Deploy

Compile to 7 targets: DOM, WebGPU, WASM, iOS, Android, Email, or Hybrid. Deploy to Vercel, Cloudflare, or Netlify.

Why Voce IR

Zero Runtime

No framework JS shipped. Spring physics solved at compile time. Every byte serves the user.

7 Compile Targets

DOM, WebGPU, WASM, Hybrid, iOS SwiftUI, Android Compose, Email HTML. One IR, every platform.

46 Validation Rules

9 passes covering structure, references, state machines, accessibility, security, SEO, forms, i18n, and motion safety.

Accessible by Default

Missing SemanticNode is a compile error. Keyboard equivalents required. Heading hierarchy enforced. ReducedMotion mandatory.

AI-Native

Not AI writing framework code faster. A system designed from scratch for AI authorship. Conversation in, production UI out.

Production Ready

Deploy to Vercel, Cloudflare, or Netlify. Real image pipeline. Font subsetting. Content-hashed assets. CSP headers.

Ready to build?

cargo install voce-validator

Voce IR. Apache-2.0 License. Built by Marc Pelland.
